summary: High‑energy Vegas show with soaring Unbroken Chain & Spanish Jam sequence
blurb: The second set shines with an unstoppable Unbroken Chain → The Other One → Days Between → Sugar Magnolia flow, while the first set is uneven but contains a solid Jack Straw and So Many Roads. Jerry delivers emotional guitar work despite hints of fatigue, and the drummers lock in for a memorable drum/space groove.
review: May 21, 1995 at the Sam Boyd Silver Bowl captured the Dead at a wild desert high point. The first set opens strong with Jack Straw and rolls through a quirky West L.A. Fadeaway, Little Red Rooster and a soulful So Many Roads that segues into The Promised Land, though several fans note it feels a bit uneven. The real fireworks erupt in the second set: Unbroken Chain launches a spell‑binding journey that carries straight into an explosive Eyes Of The World → Corinna transition, then into a marathon drum/space segment (featuring guest Carter Beauford) before blasting through The Other One, Days Between and a jubilant Sugar Magnolia. Jerry’s guitar is emotionally charged on Days Between and generally alert throughout, even as his health shows signs of strain in the mix. Phil’s bass cuts through the hissy board sound, providing a solid anchor, while Vince’s high‑pitched keys occasionally clash with vocal harmonies. The twin drummers, Mickey and Bill, are tight and inventive, delivering one of the night’s most praised rhythmic interludes. Overall, despite a few first‑set hiccups and some recording hiss, the show is remembered for its soaring second‑set improvisations and the raw, high‑energy vibe that only a Vegas desert night could inspire.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Multiple recordings praise the second‑set flow (Unbroken Chain, Spanish Jam sequence, Days Between) and tight drumming, giving strong positive sentiment. At the same time, several reviews note an uneven first set and Jerry’s fatigue, producing mixed sentiment. The net effect is a moderate positive adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Solid soundboard tape with good clarity, captures the high‑energy performance and all highlighted songs without major audio flaws.

key_highlights: Unbroken Chain launches a second‑set marathon that carries through The Other One, Days Between and Sugar Magnolia.
Spanish Jam > The Other One > Days Between sequence is cited as an explosive jam highlight.
Carter Beauford’s guest drum/space segment adds a unique rhythmic flavor.
Jerry delivers emotional guitar work on Days Between despite signs of fatigue.

band_performance.Jerry: Emotional and alert on key moments like Days Between, but some reviewers note vocal fatigue and occasional strain due to health.
band_performance.Phil: Bass remains audible even through hissy mix; provides solid foundation and shines on Unbroken Chain.
band_performance.Vince: High‑pitched keyboard voice occasionally detracts from harmonies, but overall support is solid.
band_performance.Mickey: Locks in tight grooves; part of the praised drum/space segment.
band_performance.Billy: Delivers strong rhythmic interplay with Mickey; together they drive the memorable jam sections.
